name: prefect
explanation: Prefect's check polls the Prefect Server REST API rooted at `prefect_url` (e.g. http://<host>:4200/api), hitting endpoints like /version, /health, /ready, /work_pools/filter, /events/filter. The default Prefect API server port (4200) and the `/api` prefix are stable, so a generic HTTP probe of /api/health or /api/ready on a candidate host could detect a Prefect deployment without credentials in default open-source deployments. However, Prefect Cloud and most production self-hosted deployments require an Authorization Bearer token supplied via `custom_headers`; without it the API returns 401. The integration is therefore probable on the wire (single canonical path, single port) but production reachability typically depends on user-supplied tokens, so classify as custom rather than generic.
notes: Self-hosted open-source Prefect typically runs unauthenticated on :4200/api and is reachable by a generic /api/health probe. Prefect Cloud and authenticated deployments require a bearer token supplied through custom_headers, which falls outside generic discovery.
